metadata.description: Join us for an epic day. Altorp {lapDistanceKm} km loop - "Långa gula". {dateFormatted}, {startTime}-{endTime}. As many laps as you can.

about.title: How many laps can you do?
about.text1: Join us for an epic day of pushing your limits on the beautiful "Långa gula" {lapDistanceKm} km loop in Altorp. With ~{lapElevationM}m of elevation per lap, it's a fun challenge.
about.text2: This isn't just for serious runners. Everyone is welcome. Whether you walk one lap or run twelve, the goal is to challenge yourself, enjoy the forest, and be part of an amazing community. Run, walk, rest, and see what you're capable of in {durationHours} hours.

kidsRace.title: Kids Race
kidsRace.text: This year we're introducing a kids race! A fun ~400m run, starting at 09:30 before the main event. It's free for everyone — just show up and run!

moreThanRace.title: More Than a Race
moreThanRace.subtitle: Altorp Ultra is a full day out. Bring your friends and family, hang out, and soak up the atmosphere. Good vibes, good food, and good music.
moreThanRace.beautifulSceneryDesc: Run on a stunning {lapDistanceKm}km trail loop through the Altorp forest.
moreThanRace.greatCommunityDesc: Enjoy a friendly, supportive atmosphere with fellow runners.
moreThanRace.personalChallengeDesc: Run, walk, or rest. See how far you can go in {durationHours} hours.
moreThanRace.foodDrinksDesc: Burgers, hot dogs, snacks, and drinks. Free for all runners — also available for others to purchase.
moreThanRace.musicDesc: Tunes all day long to keep the energy up — whether you're running, cheering, or just chilling.
moreThanRace.hangOutDesc: Come for the race, stay for the atmosphere. Everyone is welcome — runners, spectators, and families alike.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: Can I run only one or two laps?
faq.a1: Yes! It is all about challenging yourself. You can walk or run for as long, or short, as you want.
faq.a2: No, you can walk and rest as much as you like.
faq.a3: Yes, there will be food! For all runners snacks are included and you will be able to buy hot food (hotdogs and burgers) at a discounted price (at cost). Non-runners will be able to buy as much snacks and food as they like as well!
faq.a4: It is really easy to get here using public transport, only 12 minutes by train from Tekniska Högskolan. You can of course take your car as well, there is parking within 200m from the starting line. You can find it <link>here on Google Maps</link>.
faq.a5: The entry fee is {priceSEK} SEK. You can register and pay directly through our registration page on RaceID.

footer.description: A trail running event in the beautiful Altorp forest, Djursholm. Run, walk, or rest — see how far you can go in {durationHours} hours.

info.title: Race Information
info.intro: There is absolutely no requirement on how many laps you run — whether it's walking one lap with a stroller, running one or ten laps is entirely up to you! Everyone is welcome and there will be a wonderful mix. It's perfectly fine to do one lap and then rest for 1, 2, 3, or 4 hours and then do another one — everyone does what they want.
info.hangArea: We'll make sure there's a great hangout area at the start/finish with nice music, snacks, and barbecue!
info.startTimeText: The race starts at {startTime} and runs until {endTime}, Saturday May 9 {year}.
info.howToFindLabel: How do I find the start?
info.howToFindText1: Here is a <link>Google Maps pin</link> to where the start is.
info.howToFindText2: If you take public transport, you can get here with Roslagsbanan towards Näsbypark from Tekniska Högskolan in under 15 minutes. It's barely 200 meters to the start from the station.
info.howToFindText3: If you drive, there is parking right by the station (<link>Google Maps pin</link>).
info.paymentText: The entry fee is {priceSEK} SEK. Register and pay directly via <link>RaceID</link>.
info.formatText: As many laps as possible. There is no time limit per lap — take the time you need (this is NOT a backyard format).
info.howToWinText: The person who has completed the most laps at {endTime} wins. If multiple runners have the same number of laps, the one who finished their last lap earliest wins.
info.fluidsText: For everyone participating there will be access to water and simple snacks (chocolate bars, cinnamon buns and similar). For those who really want to push themselves all {durationHours} hours, I recommend bringing your own sports drink and gels. If you have friends and family watching, they can buy snacks at a reasonable price — water is free of course.
info.grillingText: In addition to snacks, we plan to grill burgers, hot dogs and the like that you can buy.
info.courseInfoText: Each lap is {lapDistanceKm} km and about {lapElevationM} meters of elevation. <stravaLink>Here is the route on Strava</stravaLink> and <mapsLink>here on Google Maps</mapsLink>.
info.bibText: Race numbers are distributed from 09:00 on race day.
info.equipmentText: Whatever you want! There are no specific equipment requirements.
info.valuablesText: Please bring as few valuables as possible and preferably leave them in your car or with a friend or family member who is at the event. If that's not possible, you can leave e.g. phone and wallet with us and we'll keep an eye on it during the day — if you have other needs, just let us know.
info.bagsText: Bags with sweaters, extra shoes, energy etc. can be left in the area and we will generally keep an eye on them, but we cannot be responsible for valuables in the bags.
info.paymentText2: The entry fee is {priceSEK} SEK. Register and pay directly via <link>RaceID</link>.
info.questionsText: If you have questions, just reach out to <link>{email}</link>.
